steps.title: Identify the participatory relationship
steps.content: The phrase “Falconer participates in a tradition” signals that the second sentence clarifies what the first action (exploring the sculpture) represents in a larger literary context. The transition must show this elaborative or causal link between the two sentences.

steps.title: Apply the causal transition
steps.content: ”In doing so” directly connects exploring the sculpture to participating in ekphrasis—by performing the first action, Falconer accomplishes the second. The other choices either introduce separate information (“In addition”) or signal contrast (“That said,” “Conversely”) not present in the passage. This matches choice B — B is correct.
